item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Putting a D-Class in a defiantly heroic role was really cool, especially since it didn't end in self-sacrifice for once. Really dug this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just make sure you don't get your steak from traincarguy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Originally there was gonna be a bunch of exposition at the end giving a larger slice of the backstory, but that was from a version in which the D-Class was gonna die and the spooky backstory of the area was the important thing. Obviously I ended up going a different (and better?) way with it, but the backstory is available for your perusal in the Head Cannons tab on my author page if you've a mind to do so.

And thankya for the title props! Back when the D-Class was gonna bite it, the title was going to be End of the Line. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only complaint is that I could not understand why this is accessed through an old mining train. Other than that this piece is excellently constructed. Felt like I had Crazy Train playing in the background the whole time.

Also 10/10 multi-meaning title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cheers, happy you like it.

your characterization is juicy and medium-rare, your action is followable and zesty.

And your comment is making me hungry for steak and salsa! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot of areas where I feel like this could be tightened — and a lot of areas where I started to skim. That being said, you perpetually surprised me with new directions without ever making me feel like you were actually trying to surprise me (which is quite a hard trick to pull off). I'd like to see this cleaned up a bit — but even as it is, it's an easy upvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you threw me for a couple of loops throughout this one. i thought you were gonna go one way, then this way, and you didn't do any of them, which frankly is super refreshing. your characterization is juicy and medium-rare, your action is followable and zesty. all with a nice shiny bow on top with that ending.

you did a real good one here, buckaroo. a pleasure to read and a pleasure to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D-2172 is objectively a hero for what she did. Good article. Great article. Fantastic article? Yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty goddamn excellent. Good imagery, action was on-point, and D-2172's character was fantastic.

item.title: Re: D-Class
item.content:encoded: I do have what may be a … unique headcanon on how D-Class work. I've even got a tale about it in the works at the moment! But by all means, stew away! Hopefully it'll end up a nice beef and vegetable medley. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D-2172 will return in Off the Rails 2: Trainman's Revenge!

item.title: D-Class
item.content:encoded: Not sure how I feel about the interpretation of D-Class you got here, she acted more like a field agent, which I suppose is what would warrant the possibility of promotion. Then again, I'm used to D-Class having no personality, so maybe it's just me. Sitting on neutral vote for now, let me stew on this a little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope to see more of D-2172 honestly. I like her. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was well written and unique. Not much else to say, a solid +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nope, thoughts of maternal instinct never crossed my mind. The D-Class was originally going to be a dude, actually, and he had the same reaction to the kids as the final product, but paternal instinct wasn't a factor either. D-2172 and her distaff original are just generally protective types towards anyone they feel may be unable to protect themselves.

Glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't take my snark away! It's the only thing I have going for me!

Funny thing is, originally there wasn't going to be a twist!

+ Spoilerish Rambling?
- Spoilerish Rambling.

In the beginning, I just wanted to do something simple, a spooky exploration log where a hapless D-Class gets et by a scary monster. It was right about the time said scary monster showed up that I completely lost interest in writing the article, and it took me almost two full days to figure out why. First off, it was boring me. There's no lack of stories here about just that sort of thing, and even though it was what I'd wanted to write, I realized it wasn't what I really wanted to write.

That leads to the second issue I had, which was that kind of story just not really being me. Bad guy wins, everybody gets spooped … I enjou reading that, sure, that's why I'm here, but it's not really my style of writing. When I decided hell with it, the D-Class ain't just gonna survive, she's gonna kick some gatdamned ass, the whole rest of the article just flowed out.

And yeah, the story then became less about the spooks and more about the Foundation and D-Class themselves, which I do feel works much better and is more interesting than the umpteenth guy getting his kidneys sliced into a pie.

Anywho, glad ya liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, I hate stories that have creepy kids standing at the end of hallways. Why would she go back? I guess that old saying about D-class personnel is true…

But on a serious note: did you make the D-class female to insinuate she had a maternal instinct?

Good stuff!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. I was expecting some massive twist that makes the reader have to re-conceptualize the entire article, and while we sort of got that, its low-key enough to be more impactful.

My only complaint is that sometimes there is such a thing as too snarky. But not enough in this case to change my vote.

item.title: Re: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed it
Was pretty good I thought
Not much else to add
+1

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
I can hear the train a-comin'
It's rollin' 'round the bed
And I ain't seen the sunshine
Since I don't know when

Authortorium | Imaginarium
